title: nf-meta-sample-rename pipeline parameters
description: Generalized Nextflow adapter that stages (symlinks/hardlinks/copies) the file(s) referenced by each row of a samplesheet under names rendered from a template, and writes a new samplesheet pointing at the renamed files.

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: The input samplesheet, which of its columns hold files to rename, and where/how the result is written.

samplesheet.description: Path to the input CSV/TSV samplesheet.
samplesheet.help_text: Every column is passed through to the output samplesheet unchanged except the columns named in read_cols, whose values are replaced with the renamed file's path. Delimiter is auto-detected from the file extension.
samplesheet.errorMessage: samplesheet must be a valid file path with no spaces, ending in .csv, .tsv, or .tab, and must exist.

read_cols.description: Comma-separated column=label pairs identifying which samplesheet columns hold files to rename, and the label each contributes to the '{read}' template placeholder.
read_cols.help_text: Example: 'fastq_1=R1,fastq_2=R2'. Not limited to paired-end reads -- any number of columns is supported (e.g. add 'index=I1' for an index read), and a row may leave any of these columns blank (e.g. single-end samples mixed into an otherwise paired-end sheet); a row is only an error if *all* configured columns are blank.
read_cols.errorMessage: read_cols must be a comma-separated list of column=label pairs, e.g. 'fastq_1=R1,fastq_2=R2'.

name_template.description: Output filename template, or the name of a built-in preset.
name_template.help_text: A value containing '{' is used as a literal template, e.g. '{sample}_{read}.fastq.gz' -- placeholders are any samplesheet column name, plus the synthetic '{read}' placeholder (the label configured for whichever read_cols column a given file came from). A value with no '{' is looked up as a preset name instead; currently only '10x_mkfastq' ('{sample}_S1_L001_{read}_001.fastq.gz') is built in.

outdir.description: Directory the renamed files and output samplesheet are written under.
outdir.help_text: Renamed files are written to '<outdir>/samples/' and the output samplesheet to '<outdir>/samplesheet/'.

outname.help_text: The extension controls the output format: '.csv' writes comma-delimited, '.tsv'/'.tab' writes tab-delimited.

quote.description: Quote character used both when parsing the input samplesheet and when quoting output fields.

staging_options.title: Staging options
staging_options.description: How each renamed file relates to its original.

file_op.description: How to stage each file under its rendered name.
file_op.help_text: 'symlink' (default) creates a symlink to the original -- fast, no data duplication. 'link' creates a hardlink -- same filesystem only, but behaves as a real file for tools that refuse to follow symlinks. 'copy' duplicates the file's contents -- needed when crossing storage backends.
file_op.errorMessage: file_op must be one of: symlink, link, copy.
